title: Cell culture protocol for sc-Tiling: High-resolution characterization of gene function using single-cell CRISPR tiling
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"hlond","text":"Identification of novel functional domains and characterization of detailed regulatory mechanisms in cancer-driving genes is critical for advanced cancer therapy. Here, we present the cell culture protocol for “sc-Tiling,” which integrates a CRISPR gene-tiling screen with 10X Genomics single-cell RNAseq (sc-RNAseq) workflow.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"q2vhk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rybva","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"an3an","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Blasticidin (Blast) for LentiCas9-Blast selection: titration range 0, 0.625, 1.25, 2.5, 5, 10, 20, 40 ug/ml final x 3-5 days, find the minimal killing dose.
Puromycin (Puro) for sgRNA library selection: titration range 0, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 2, 4 ug/ml final x 2-3 days, find the minimal killing dose.

We use LentiCas9-Blast stably transduced cells for a constitutive spCas9 expression. We suggest single cloning these cells and identify monoclonal Cas9 + cells that exert the best CRISPR editing activity for the CRISPR library screen. For this, we developed a “self-inactivation RFP system” for a flow cytometric assay to select the best CRISPR editing clones.
Transduce the cells with LentiCas9-Blast at ~15-20% infection. Blasticidin selects for 3-5 days.
Single cloning by seeding the cells in Blast-medium in 96-well-plates x 3, each contains 300, 100, and 30 cells in the plate.
Select wells that contain monoclonal cells and expand the culture. It typically takes 2 – 4 weeks to expand from a single cell to get enough cells for frozen stocks and the functional test.
Test the CRISPR editing efficiency of each monoclonal Cas9-expressing cell using the sgRNA targeting RFP (sgRFP; 5’-GTCACCACATACGAAGACGG-3’). Select the best CRISPR editing clones for CRISPR screens.

Make library virus using HEK293 cells in P100 dishes. We suggest making 10 dishes of virus per 1,000 sgRNA constructs in the library for a typical one condition, 1,000x coverage, triplicated screen. The real dish # of viral production required for the screen depends on the infection potential of the target cells, and the screen designs such as coverage-fold, # of replicates, # of conditions (e.g., drug treatments, etc.). A pilot culture to calculate the required viral amount is highly recommended.
Day 1: Seed HEK293 cells at 3.4 x 10 6 cells per P100 dish in DMEM medium with 10% FBS, penicillin (100 units/ml), streptomycin (100 μg/ml), and plasmocin (5 μg/ml).
Day 2: Transfect the HEK293 cells with a mixture of pPAX2 plasmid: pMD2 plasmid: CRISPR library plasmid = 5.6 μg: 5.6 μg: 5.6 μg (Total DNA = 17 μg).
Day 3: Replace medium 18-24 h after transfection.
Day 5: Harvest the viral containing supernatant from the HEK293 cultures. Spin at 2,000 g x 10 min to remove the cell debris. Aliquot the viral supernatant and store @ -80℃.

Setup a viral titration in a 12-well plate scale. Typical seeding density: 1 – 2 million cells per 12-well. Titrate the virus with a 2-fold gradience. Each well contains 10ug/mL polybrene (final concentration).

Read the RFP+ cell % at 48hr post-infection. Identify the well with 15 – 20 % RFP + % (this will be the titer for the real screen). Using this condition, we expect to have ~ 90% of the infected cells receiving only one sgRNA construct.

Transduce the Cas9-expressing cells with the sgRNA library virus using the condition for 15 – 20 % RFP + infection and add puromycin 24hr post-infection (and maintain puromycin during the whole course of the screen).
After 24hr in puromycin, check the RFP + % (usually expect to reach > 60% RFP + after 24hr puromycin treatment). This is the START of the test screen culture.
Seed cells in the culture conditions for the screen. If planning for a drug response screen, use this Pilot test culture to titrate for the drug concentrations as needed.

Use the pilot library test culture to determine the large-scale screen culture protocol (days, seeding density, drug conditions…). Calculate the reagents required for the large-scale screen culture (virus, medium, compounds, FBS, cytokines, etc.) to ensure sufficient materials to complete the screen.
Transduced at 15 – 20% RFP + as previously titrated.

Initial sgRNA library infection needs to get 1,000x coverage to get enough cells to start. Example for a 1,000 sgRNA library: Start from 10 million cells per replicate at 15~20% infection rate. Expecting at least 1 million independently infected RFP + cells.
Add puromycin 24hr after infection.
At the defined time point (e.g., day 3 post-transduction), submit freshly harvested screen cells for 10X Chromium single-cell isolation using the 10X GEM v3.1 kit.
